project.title: HUVEC Palmitoylation modification analysis
project.projectDescription: Detection of palmitoylated proteins using HUVEC cells treated with BSA or oleic acid
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The treatment group treated HUVEC cells with 200 μM oleic acid for 12 hours, and the control group treated HUVEC cells with the same concentration of BSA for 12 hours. The cells were collected and frozen at -80°C for detection.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Reversed phase column information: C18 column (1.8μm, 0.15×1,00mm ) Chromatographic instrument: Thermo Fisher Easy-nLC 1000 Mass spectrometer: Thermo Fisher LTQ Obitrap ETD Chromatographic separation time: 98min Solution A: 0.1% formic acid in water Solution B: 100% acetonitrile solution Flow rate: 600nL/min The original mass spectrometry data were in RAW format and were searched and analyzed using the software Proteome Discoverer 1.4 (Thermo). Data used this time: uniprot-Bifidobacterium bifidum ATCC 29521.fasta Search the database using Proteome Discoverer 1.4 (Thermo)

references.referenceLine: Tan Y, Huang Z, Jin Y, Wang J, Fan H, Liu Y, Zhang L, Wu Y, Liu P, Li T, Ran J, Tian H, Lam SM, Liu M, Zhou J, Yang Y. Lipid droplets sequester palmitic acid to disrupt endothelial ciliation and exacerbate atherosclerosis in male mice. Nat Commun. 2024 15(1):8273

Comment[Instrument]: liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) with an EASY-nLC 1000 ultraperformance liquid chromatography (UPLC) system connected to an LTQ Velos mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: Mass spectra were acquired in a data-dependent manner
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: mass tolerance for MS1 set at 20 ppm
